creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
Might it be possible to upgrade to 2.1 at this point?

If there are compatibility issues, how about a py-networkx2 port?

I'm working on a port of the MARVEL genome assembler, which requires
py36-networkx 2.1.

checking for python3.6 module networkx... yes
checking for version 2.1 or higher of networkx... no
configure: error: You need at least version 2.1 of the networkx Python module.
===>  Script "configure" failed unexpectedly.
Please report the problem to jwb@FreeBSD.org [maintainer] and attach the
"/usr/ports/wip/marvel/work/MARVEL-1f693baf8420c2121cc40d18ed2088c6e81a713b/config.log"
including the output of the failure of your make command. Also, it might be
a good idea to provide an overview of all packages installed on your system
(e.g. a /usr/local/sbin/pkg-static info -g -Ea).
*** Error code 1

Thanks...
